question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget authorization check] Can an attacker call supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget with taker=victim under the precondition that isAuthorized[victim][attacker] == false, triggering [vm.prank(attacker); bundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget(..., taker=victim, receiver=attacker, ...)], violating the invariant that only taker or authorized agents can initiate a sell bundle, causing scoped impact: attacker forces victim into a debt position and routes sellerAssets to attacker-controlled receiver? Proof idea: unit test with unauthorized caller and assert Unauthorized() revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: MidnightBundles authorization + Midnight isAuthorized two-layer check] Can an attacker who is authorized by taker on Midnight (isAuthorized[taker][attacker] == true) but where taker has NOT authorized the bundler (isAuthorized[taker][bundler] == false) call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral under the precondition that bundler's withdrawCollateral call requires isAuthorized[taker][bundler], triggering [attacker.bundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral(taker=victim, ...)], violating the invariant that both the caller-to-bundler and bundler-to-Midnight authorization layers must be satisfied, causing scoped impact: partial execution — takes succeed but withdrawCollateral reverts, leaving taker in unexpected debt state? Proof idea: state test with isAuthorized[taker][attacker]=true but isAuthorized[taker][bundler]=false and assert withdrawCollateral step reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: MidnightBundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral authorization] Can an attacker call repayAndWithdrawCollateral(market, assets, onBehalf=victim, ...) under the precondition that isAuthorized[victim][attacker] == false, triggering [vm.prank(attacker); bundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral(market, assets, victim, ...)], violating the invariant that only onBehalf or authorized agents can repay and withdraw collateral on behalf of another user, causing scoped impact: unauthorized collateral withdrawal after forced repayment? Proof idea: unit test with unauthorized caller and assert Unauthorized() revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setConsumed authorization] Can an attacker call setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, victim) under the precondition that isAuthorized[victim][attacker] == false, triggering [vm.prank(attacker); midnight.setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, victim)], violating the invariant that only victim or authorized agents can cancel victim's offers via setConsumed, causing scoped impact: unauthorized offer cancellation — all of victim's offers in the group are permanently cancelled? Proof idea: unit test with unauthorized caller and assert Unauthorized() revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setConsumed by authorized agent cancels maker offers] Can an authorized agent B call setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, maker_A) under the precondition that isAuthorized[A][B] == true and A has active offers in group, triggering [vm.prank(B); midnight.setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, A)], violating the invariant that offer cancellation should require explicit maker consent, causing scoped impact: authorized agent can unilaterally cancel all of maker's offers in a group, disrupting market-making strategies? Proof idea: state test authorized agent cancels maker's group and assert subsequent take reverts with ConsumedUnits/ConsumedAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: claimSettlementFee + fee-on-transfer token] Can the feeClaimer call claimSettlementFee(feeOnTransferToken, amount, receiver) under the precondition that claimableSettlementFee[feeOnTransferToken] == amount and the token charges a transfer fee, triggering [feeClaimer.claimSettlementFee(feeOnTransferToken, amount, receiver)], violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee[token] -= amount must match actual tokens transferred to receiver, causing scoped impact: accounting desync — claimableSettlementFee decremented by amount but receiver gets amount - fee, leaving protocol balance understated? Proof idea: unit test with fee-on-transfer token and assert receiver balance < amount after claim.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: claimContinuousFee accounting invariant] Can the feeClaimer call claimContinuousFee(market, amount, receiver) where amount > actual accrued fee but amount <= continuousFeeCredit under the precondition that continuousFeeCredit was inflated by a large credit position with high continuousFee, triggering [feeClaimer.claimContinuousFee(market, continuousFeeCredit, receiver)], violating the invariant that claimed continuous fees must not exceed fees actually accrued from lenders' pendingFee, causing scoped impact: direct loss — feeClaimer extracts more than earned fees, reducing lender-redeemable withdrawable? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee index boundary] Can the feeSetter call setMarketSettlementFee(id, 7, fee) under the precondition that index == 7 > 6, triggering [feeSetter.setMarketSettlementFee(id, 7, 0)], violating the invariant that index must be <= 6 (InvalidFeeIndex), causing scoped impact: revert — but fuzz index in [0, type(uint256).max] and assert only [0,6] succeed? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee CBP alignment] Can the feeSetter call setMarketSettlementFee(id, index, fee) where fee % CBP != 0 under the precondition that fee is a valid amount but not a multiple of 1e12, triggering [feeSetter.setMarketSettlementFee(id, 1, 1e12 + 1)], violating the invariant that settlement fees must be multiples of CBP (1e12) to fit in uint16 cbp storage, causing scoped impact: revert with FeeNotMultipleOfFeeCbp — but fuzz fee in [0, maxSettlementFee(index)] and assert only CBP-aligned values succeed? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee on non-existent market] Can the feeSetter call setMarketSettlementFee(nonExistentId, 0, 0) under the precondition that marketState[nonExistentId].tickSpacing == 0, triggering [feeSetter.setMarketSettlementFee(bytes32(0), 0, 0)], violating the invariant that fee changes must only apply to created markets, causing scoped impact: revert with MarketNotCreated — but confirm no state change occurs for non-existent market IDs? Proof idea: unit test setMarketSettlementFee on uncreated market and assert MarketNotCreated() revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: multicall + setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker use multicall to atomically call [setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, victim), withdraw(market, units, victim, attacker)] under the precondition that attacker is already authorized by victim (isAuthorized[victim][attacker] == true), triggering a single multicall transaction, violating the invariant that multicall must not allow authorization escalation followed by immediate fund extraction in one atomic call, causing scoped impact: attacker drains victim's credit in a single transaction? Proof idea: unit test multicall([setIsAuthorized, withdraw]) and assert Unauthorized() on setIsAuthorized step since attacker != victim.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketContinuousFee retroactive pendingFee desync] Can the feeSetter call setMarketContinuousFee(id, 0) after lenders have accumulated pendingFee under the precondition that lenders have credit > 0 and pendingFee > 0, triggering [feeSetter.setMarketContinuousFee(id, 0); lender.withdraw(market, credit, lender, lender)], violating the invariant that pendingFee already accrued must still be deducted from credit on withdrawal even after fee rate is set to zero, causing scoped impact: accounting desync — lender withdraws more credit than their net position (credit - pendingFee) if pendingFee is not properly tracked? Proof idea: state test set fee to 0 mid-market and assert lender net credit == credit - pendingFee on withdrawal.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee + take price impact] Can the feeSetter call setMarketSettlementFee(id, 0, MAX_SETTLEMENT_FEE_0_DAYS) immediately before maturity under the precondition that existing buy offers have offerPrice close to the new settlementFee, triggering [feeSetter.setMarketSettlementFee(id, 0, max); taker.take(offer, ...)], violating the invariant that takers must not be forced into a revert or negative sellerAssets due to a fee change between offer signing and fill, causing scoped impact: implicit offer cancellation — all buy offers with price <= new settlementFee become unfillable? Proof idea: state test increase settlement fee to match offer tick price and assert take reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: claimContinuousFee totalUnits/withdrawable underflow] Can the feeClaimer call claimContinuousFee(market, amount, receiver) where amount > withdrawable under the precondition that continuousFeeCredit > withdrawable (possible if bad debt reduced withdrawable but not continuousFeeCredit proportionally), triggering [feeClaimer.claimContinuousFee(market, continuousFeeCredit, receiver)], violating the invariant that withdrawable >= continuousFeeCredit must hold at all times, causing scoped impact: arithmetic underflow revert or accounting corruption if invariant is violated? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized self-authorization] Can a user call setIsAuthorized(user, true, user) under the precondition that onBehalf == msg.sender, triggering [vm.prank(user); midnight.setIsAuthorized(user, true, user)], violating the invariant that self-authorization is a no-op (user is always authorized for themselves), causing scoped impact: no direct impact but confirm isAuthorized[user][user] == true does not create unexpected behavior in authorization checks that use isAuthorized[onBehalf][msg.sender]? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: MidnightBundles referralFeePct boundary] Can an attacker call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with referralFeePct = WAD - 1 under the precondition that filledBuyerAssets > 0, triggering [bundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral(..., referralFeePct=WAD-1, referralFeeRecipient=attacker)], violating the invariant that referralFeeAssets must not exceed filledBuyerAssets, causing scoped impact: near-total extraction of buyer's filled assets as referral fee to attacker-controlled recipient, leaving msg.sender with near-zero refund? Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct near WAD and assert msg.sender refund == maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets >= 0.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget receiver parameter] Can an attacker call supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget with receiver=attacker and taker=victim under the precondition that isAuthorized[victim][attacker] == true on Midnight, triggering [vm.prank(attacker); bundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget(..., taker=victim, receiver=attacker, ...)], violating the invariant that sellerAssets must flow to the taker or an address explicitly chosen by the taker, causing scoped impact: attacker routes victim's sellerAssets to attacker's own address? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketTickSpacing + existing offers] Can the tickSpacingSetter call setMarketTickSpacing(id, 1) under the precondition that existing signed offers have tick values that were previously inaccessible (tick % 4 != 0 but tick % 1 == 0), triggering [tickSpacingSetter.setMarketTickSpacing(id, 1); taker.take(offer_with_previously_invalid_tick, ...)], violating the invariant that tick accessibility must be consistent with the tick spacing at offer signing time, causing scoped impact: offers at previously invalid ticks become fillable after tick spacing decrease, potentially at prices makers did not intend to offer? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized + withdraw race condition] Can an authorized agent B call setIsAuthorized(B, false, A) to revoke their own authorization and then immediately call withdraw(market, units, A, B) in the same multicall under the precondition that multicall uses delegatecall preserving msg.sender, triggering [multicall([setIsAuthorized(B, false, A), withdraw(market, units, A, B)])], violating the invariant that authorization state at the time of each sub-call must be respected, causing scoped impact: withdraw succeeds or fails depending on ordering — confirm multicall processes authorization revocation before withdraw? Proof idea: unit test multicall([revoke_self, withdraw]) and assert withdraw reverts with Unauthorized.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: claimSettlementFee cross-token] Can the feeClaimer call claimSettlementFee(tokenA, amount, receiver) where claimableSettlementFee[tokenA] was accumulated from a market with loanToken == tokenB under the precondition that claimableSettlementFee mapping is keyed by token address, triggering [feeClaimer.claimSettlementFee(tokenA, claimableSettlementFee[tokenA], receiver)], violating the invariant that settlement fees for tokenA must only be claimable up to the amount accumulated from tokenA markets, causing scoped impact: confirm no cross-token fee confusion — claimableSettlementFee[tokenA] is only incremented by take() calls in markets where loanToken == tokenA? Proof idea: invariant test asserting claimableSettlementFee[token] == sum of (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) across all takes in markets with loanToken == token.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setDefaultSettlementFee + touchMarket race] Can the feeSetter call setDefaultSettlementFee(loanToken, index, newFee) between a market creator's touchMarket call being submitted and mined under the precondition that market does not yet exist and feeSetter front-runs the touchMarket, triggering [feeSetter.setDefaultSettlementFee(loanToken, 6, MAX_SETTLEMENT_FEE_360_DAYS); creator.touchMarket(market)], violating the invariant that market creators should receive the fee schedule they expected at market creation time, causing scoped impact: newly created market inherits max settlement fees, harming all takers in that market? Proof idea: state test front-run default fee change before touchMarket and assert market inherits new (higher) fees.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral referralFee accounting] Can an attacker call buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with referralFeePct > 0 under the precondition that targetBuyerAssets is small and referralFeeAssets rounds down to 0 via mulDivDown, triggering [bundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral(targetBuyerAssets=1, ..., referralFeePct=WAD/2, referralFeeRecipient=attacker)], violating the invariant that referralFeeAssets == 0 when mulDivDown rounds to zero but targetFilledBuyerAssets == targetBuyerAssets, causing scoped impact: referral fee recipient receives 0 but full targetBuyerAssets is consumed — confirm no dust loss or accounting error? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized + EcrecoverAuthorizer combined] Can an attacker use EcrecoverAuthorizer to set isAuthorized[victim][ecrecoverAuthorizer] = false (revoke) under the precondition that victim previously authorized ecrecoverAuthorizer and attacker has a valid signed Authorization{isAuthorized: false} from victim, triggering [ecrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized(auth{isAuthorized: false}, sig)], violating the invariant that only the authorizer should be able to revoke their own authorizations, causing scoped impact: attacker with victim's signed revocation message can permanently disable victim's ability to use EcrecoverAuthorizer for future authorizations? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketContinuousFee + claimContinuousFee ordering] Can the feeSetter call setMarketContinuousFee(id, 0) immediately before feeClaimer calls claimContinuousFee under the precondition that continuousFeeCredit > 0 and feeSetter front-runs feeClaimer, triggering [feeSetter.setMarketContinuousFee(id, 0); feeClaimer.claimContinuousFee(market, continuousFeeCredit, receiver)], violating the invariant that setting continuousFee to 0 must not affect already-accrued continuousFeeCredit, causing scoped impact: confirm continuousFeeCredit is unchanged by setMarketContinuousFee — feeClaimer can still claim previously accrued fees? Proof idea: state test set fee to 0 and assert continuousFeeCredit unchanged, then claim succeeds.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: MidnightBundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral referralFee + units mismatch] Can an attacker call repayAndWithdrawCollateral(market, assets, onBehalf, ..., referralFeePct=WAD/2) under the precondition that units = assets - referralFeeAssets = assets/2 but borrower's debt == assets, triggering [bundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral(market, assets, onBehalf, ..., referralFeePct=WAD/2)], violating the invariant that repaid units must equal the borrower's full debt to enable full collateral withdrawal, causing scoped impact: partial repayment (units = assets/2) leaves residual debt, collateral withdrawal reverts with UnhealthyBorrower, but referralFee is already paid — attacker extracts referral fee without completing repayment? Proof idea: unit test with referralFeePct = WAD/2 and assert collateral withdrawal fails while referral fee is transferred.
